heading: Definitions
label: 10101 Definitions
notes: Historical and Revision NotesRevised sectionSource section (U.S. Code)1010146:713Section 10101 defines the terms master, seaman, and owner as they apply to merchant seamen’s protection and relief.

children.text: “master” means the individual having command of a vessel.
“owner” means the person to whom the vessel belongs.
“seaman” means an individual (except scientific personnel, a sailing school instructor, or a sailing school student) engaged or employed in any capacity on board a vessel.

children.label: B
children.text: a fish processing vessel entered into service before , and not more than 1,600 gross tons as measured under , or an alternate tonnage measured under as prescribed by the Secretary under or entered into service after , and having not more than 16 individuals on board primarily employed in the preparation of fish or fish products.January 1, 1988December 31, 1987section 14502 of this titlesection 14302 of this titlesection 14104 of this title
